tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hey, I wanted to mention something for future reference - I really prefer projecting classic 35mm films at 24 frames per second with the xenon lamp set to around 85% brightness. That gives me the best balance between image quality and keeping the film in good condition. Could you make a note of that for me?

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "User prefers projecting classic 35mm films at 24 frames per second with xenon lamp set to around 85% brightness. They state this gives the best balance between image quality and keeping the film in good condition."}

messages.content: {"status": "success", "tool": "add_memory", "memory_id": "mem_8f3d2a1c", "stored_text": "User prefers projecting classic 35mm films at 24 frames per second with xenon lamp set to around 85% brightness. They state this gives the best balance between image quality and keeping the film in good condition."}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content: Got it! I've noted your preference for projecting classic 35mm films at 24 frames per second with the xenon lamp at around 85% brightness. I'll keep that in mind for future discussions about film projection!
Hey, could you remind me what projection settings I mentioned before? I want to make sure I'm sticking with those preferences for my classic films.

function_call.arguments: {"query": "35mm film projection settings xenon lamp brightness frames per second", "limit": 3}

They state this gives the best balance between image quality and keeping the film in good condition.", "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"}], "results_count": 1}

- **Xenon lamp brightness:** Around 85%

messages.content:
Currently there's just one memory saved - the projection settings you mentioned:

- **35mm film projection:** Xenon lamp at high brightness (around 85%), 24 frames per second

That's the only note stored so far. If there's anything else you'd like me to remember about your preferences or anything else, just let me know!
Hey, since that's the only thing stored and I've already reviewed it, could you go ahead and delete that note? I've got the settings memorized now and don't really need it taking up space anymore.
Done! I've deleted that note about your projection settings. Let me know if there's anything else you'd like me to remember or if you need help with anything else!

That gives me the best balance between image quality and keeping the film in good condition. Could you make a note of that for me?
